cross_domain_targets.summary: Public city-side campaign folder title resolves to Rachel Kertz's 2024 City Council candidate committee.
Discovery-stage candidacy candidate tying Rachel Kertz, the District 4 seat, and the 2024 San Rafael general election page.
Form 460 filing listed in the public Rachel Kertz 2024 campaign folder.
Form 497 late-contribution filing listed in the public Rachel Kertz 2024 campaign folder.

not_implied: The article quote about sanctioned camping does not imply that the listed campaign filings funded or drove that policy stance.
This example demonstrates actor continuity across media and campaign layers, not a causal claim about donations, expenditures, or policy influence.

overlap_examples.notes: This is the first direct media-to-campaign overlap in the repo. It became possible only after the public Laserfiche folder-listing path exposed real committee and filing rows for city-side San Rafael campaign folders.

methodology_findings.summary: A campaign overlap becomes defensible when the public filing surface exposes a candidate-linked committee title and filing rows for the same canonical actor already joined from the media layer.
The first clean overlap is actor continuity plus campaign infrastructure, not issue-causality. The graph should preserve that boundary explicitly.
Page-linked public-records folder listings are strong enough to promote committee and filing objects even before raw filing PDFs are captured.

notes: This worked example intentionally uses a public officeholder who is both quoted in media and present in the city-side campaign filing layer.
The overlap remains bounded to identity, office, committee, and filing continuity.
